title: The Great Peg Escape
tagline: Stuck in the sofa cushions, no one can hear you roll.
synopsis: A brave yellow peg person must navigate the perilous fabric canyons of the living room sofa to avoid being swallowed by a golden retriever. Lacking limbs, the hero performs a series of daring rolls to reach the safety of the toy chest.
fun_fact: The stunt-double for the hero had to be retired because it sustained a severe paint-chip on its nose during the 'sofa cushion canyon' tumble.

title: Love at the Plug Hole
tagline: She had the perfect chassis, he had the perfect peg base.
synopsis: In a town built entirely of modular plastic roads, a blue school bus driver falls for a passenger who fits perfectly into his center-seat plug hole. Their relationship is tested when the toddler-god decides to swap the bus body for a fire engine.
fun_fact: The actors had to be superglued to the vehicle base for the kissing scene because they kept tipping over during the romantic monologue.

title: The Vacuum of Doom
tagline: The roar you hear is the end of the nursery.
synopsis: The house is quiet until the giant mechanical beast begins its deafening roar, sucking away everything in its path. One lone green peg-man realizes that the only way to survive the vacuum is to wedged himself into the corner of the wooden toy box.
fun_fact: The vacuum cleaner prop was actually a giant metal trash can painted silver, which made the actors nervous because of the echo.

title: Pegs of the Old West
tagline: This shelf ain't big enough for both of us.
synopsis: A sheriff with a painted-on star tries to maintain order in a cardboard town when a drifter with a mysterious molded-plastic hat rolls into the dusty nursery floor. Without hands to hold six-shooters, they must rely on intense, unmoving staring contests.
fun_fact: The 'dust' on the set was actually finely ground oatmeal, which caused a massive infestation of tiny ants that tried to carry the actors away.

title: The Great Plastic Migration
tagline: A journey of ten thousand inches starts with a single wobble.
synopsis: This gripping documentary follows a cluster of retired peg people as they navigate the treacherous migration from the bottom of the closet to the high-shelf collector's display. It highlights the struggle of maintaining a straight, upright posture when your base has become worn and rounded.
fun_fact: The film crew used high-speed cameras to capture the moment a peg person lost their balance, taking three months of footage to get the perfect 'slow-tip' shot.

title: Love at the Plug-Hole
tagline: A connection that just doesn't fit.
synopsis: A blue-hatted Nurse and a red-hatted Doctor share a longing glance across the kitchen floor, but their rigid cylindrical shapes make physical intimacy impossible without someone physically picking them up to touch heads. Will they ever find a custom-molded base that allows them to hold hands?
fun_fact: The love scene took three days to film because the props department kept accidentally swapping the red hat for a green one, causing severe continuity errors.

title: The Vacuum Menace
tagline: The roar of the motor is the sound of your doom.
synopsis: An entire neighborhood of plastic peg-people watches in terror as a thundering mechanical beast with a rotating brush-roll begins swallowing everything in its path. With no limbs to hold onto the furniture, the residents must find a way to wedge themselves into the safety of a non-removable baseplate before they are sucked into the dust bin.
fun_fact: The 'vacuum' sound effect was actually just a crew member humming loudly into a plastic cup while shaking a box of dry cereal.

title: Pegs in Space
tagline: To reach the top of the shelf, you must go beyond.
synopsis: In a future where the living room is a vast galaxy, a brave Astronaut pilot discovers that his vehicle's plastic steering wheel is purely decorative and he cannot actually turn the craft. He must rely on the giant hand of fate—a toddler—to steer his ship toward the final frontier: the toy box.
fun_fact: The 'stars' in the background were actually just glitter glued to a piece of black construction paper that kept falling down every five minutes.

title: The Long Range Walk
tagline: It's just five inches, but it feels like a marathon.
synopsis: A weary Sheriff with a painted-on mustache rides his sturdy wooden horse across the scorching plains of the hardwood floor. He faces off against a band of outlaws who have maliciously tipped over every plastic fence in town, forcing him to stand perfectly still for hours in the midday sun.
fun_fact: The horse was cast from a vintage mold, but the Sheriff kept falling off the saddle because his peg-hole was slightly too shallow for the horse's mounting post.
